evals.prompt: Set up a new full-stack app called 'expense-tracker' with a Nuxt frontend and FastAPI backend.
evals.expected_output: The assistant runs `uv run python scripts/create.py expense-tracker`, explains what the script creates (Nuxt frontend with bun, FastAPI backend with uv), and provides the dev startup commands for both frontend and backend.
expectations: The assistant runs scripts/create.py with expense-tracker as the project name
bun is used for the frontend (not npm or yarn)
uv is used for the backend (not pip)
The assistant provides the frontend dev command: cd expense-tracker/frontend && bun run dev
The assistant provides the backend dev command: cd expense-tracker/backend && uv run dev

evals.prompt: After scaffolding a new app, how do I install the companion skills so the AI has context on the stack?
evals.expected_output: The assistant runs `uv run python scripts/install-skills.py` and explains the four skills it installs (nuxt-ui, nuxt, frontend-design, fastapi-templates) and where they are installed (.agents/skills/, .agent/skills/, .claude/skills/).
expectations: The four installed skills are named: nuxt-ui, nuxt, frontend-design, fastapi-templates
The install locations (.agents/skills etc.) are mentioned
